## Overview

## Visa Fees

[Table:   D1 - Subordinate Work Activity]
Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Exemptions on National Visa

[Table:   E1 - Medical Treatment]
* Direct descendants of residence permit holders, in the framework of the family reunification legal provisions. Spouse pays fee. 

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Please click here (#)
Please click here to know more about the documents required - English 
Important Note: Applicants who will be sponsored will require to submit the Termo de Responsabilidade. It is mandatory to fully and properly fill the form with authorized signatures by the person sponsoring the visa applicant then notarized by the accredited authorities in Portugal is mandatory.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:

General The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is
                                                                                        not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels
                                                                                        one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the
                                                                                        picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning
                                                                                            will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                                    to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with. Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application is approximately 90 days, unless otherwise stipulated by the Portugal Embassy depending on the supporting documents submitted.

## Download Form
Click here for filing a visa application form.

[Table:   Tourist]
Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Exemptions on Schengen Visa (GRATIS)

[Table:   Business]
School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers who are nationals of third countries traveling for the purpose of scientific research in the Community as defined in Recommendation 2005/761 / EC of the European Parliament
                                                                        and of the Council of 28 September, to facilitate the issuance by Member States of short-term uniform visas for researchers from third countries who are traveling for the purposes
                                                                        of scientific research in the Community.
Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
                                                                    
NOTE: NO SERVICE CHARGE/SERVICE FEE SHALL BE LEVIED FOR THE CATEGORIES WHO FALL UNDER GRATIS CATEGORY.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for an application at the Embassy of Portugal is a minimum of 15 calendar days. Please note that all those applying for a visa for Portugal need to have at least 15
                                                                        days between the date of application and date of travel. Applications will not be accepted if this requirement is not adhered to.
In addition to the above processing time please refer to Article 22:
Prior consultation of central authorities of other Member States
- A Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation shall not apply to applications for airport transit visas.
- The central authorities consulted shall reply definitively within seven calendar days after being consulted. The absence of a reply within this deadline shall mean that they have no grounds for objecting to the issuing of the visa.
- Member States shall notify the Commission of the introduction or withdrawal of the requirement of prior consultation before it becomes applicable. This information shall also be given within local Schengen cooperation in the jurisdiction concerned.
- The Commission shall inform Member States of such notifications.
- From the date of the replacement of the Schengen Consultation Network, as referred to in Article 46 of the VIS Regulation, prior consultation shall be carried out in accordance with Article 16(2) of that Regulation.
Click here for the list of Countries that article 22 apply to.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
 Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences

## Documents Required

Please click here to know more about the documents required 
Applicants who will be sponsored will require to submit the Termo de Responsabilidade. It is mandatory to fully and properly fill the form with authorized signatures by the person sponsoring the visa applicant. 
Sponsors of Domestic helpers will require to submit the Termo de Responsabilidade. It is mandatory to fully and properly fill the form with authorized signatures by the local sponsor in UAE.

Please note the authentication of signature can be done at the Portugal Embassy in Abu Dhabi or by authorise authorities only.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.

Such consultation shall not apply to applications for airport transit visas.
- The central authorities consulted shall reply definitively within seven calendar days after being consulted. The absence of a reply within this deadline shall mean that they have no grounds for objecting to the issuing of the visa.
- Member States shall notify the Commission of the introduction or withdrawal of the requirement of prior consultation before it becomes applicable. This information shall also be given within local Schengen cooperation in the jurisdiction concerned.
- The Commission shall inform Member States of such notifications.
- From the date of the replacement of the Schengen Consultation Network, as referred to in Article 46 of the VIS Regulation, prior consultation shall be carried out in accordance with Article 16(2) of that Regulation.

Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Tourist]
Please be informed that the fees charged by the Embassy or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
Exemptions on Schengen Visa (GRATIS)

[Table:   Medical Treatment]
School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
Researchers who are nationals of third countries traveling for the purpose of scientific research in the Community as defined in Recommendation 2005/761 / EC of the European Parliament
                                                                        and of the Council of 28 September, to facilitate the issuance by Member States of short-term uniform visas for researchers from third countries who are traveling for the purposes
                                                                        of scientific research in the Community.
Representatives of non-profit organizations aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organized by non-profit organizations.
                                                                    
NOTE: NO SERVICE CHARGE/SERVICE FEE SHALL BE LEVIED FOR THE CATEGORIES WHO FALL UNDER GRATIS CATEGORY.

## Documents Required
Please click here to know more about the documents required 
Applicants who will be sponsored will require to submit the Termo de Responsabilidade. It is mandatory to fully and properly fill the form with authorized signatures by the person sponsoring the visa applicant. 
Sponsors of Domestic helpers will require to submit the Termo de Responsabilidade. It is mandatory to fully and properly fill the form with authorized signatures by the local sponsor in UAE.
Please note the authentication of signature can be done at the Portugal Embassy in Abu Dhabi or by authorise authorities only.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The processing time for an application at the Embassy of Portugal is a minimum of 15 calendar days. Please note that all those applying for a visa for Portugal need to have at least 15
                                                                        days between the date of application and date of travel. Applications will not be accepted if this requirement is not adhered to.
In addition to the above processing time please refer to Article 22:
Prior consultation of central authorities of other Member States
- A Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation shall not apply to applications for airport transit visas.
- The central authorities consulted shall reply definitively within seven calendar days after being consulted. The absence of a reply within this deadline shall mean that they have no grounds for objecting to the issuing of the visa.
- Member States shall notify the Commission of the introduction or withdrawal of the requirement of prior consultation before it becomes applicable. This information shall also be given within local Schengen cooperation in the jurisdiction concerned.
- The Commission shall inform Member States of such notifications.
- From the date of the replacement of the Schengen Consultation Network, as referred to in Article 46 of the VIS Regulation, prior consultation shall be carried out in accordance with Article 16(2) of that Regulation.
Click here for the list of Countries that article 22 apply to.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
 Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences 

## Download Form
Please click here to fill the Visa form 
Important Note: Handwritten forms are not accepted

Schengen Visa
Tourist (#)
Business (#)
Medical Treatment (#)
Training Purpose (#)
Airport Transit (#)
All applications have to be made in person by the applicant due to the biometric enrolment process. All applicants must fully comply with relevant documentation required for respective visa categories. Incomplete applications may not be accepted.
Applicants are free to apply up to 90 days prior to their date of travel. The Embassy recommends that you apply at least 3 weeks prior to your date of travel to accommodate any unforeseen processing
                                                                delays. 
You may submit your visa application at the nearest VFS Global Portugal Visa Application Centre. Those applying for short-stay Schengen visas are advised to submit their application at the Portugal
                                                                Visa Application Centre.
                                                            
 The Convention implementing the Schengen Agreement came into force on 26 March 1995. The Schengen countries require visitors to obtain a common visa, which permits entry to the Schengen Area.

Important Note: Applicants who will be sponsored will require to submit the Termo de Responsabilidade. It is mandatory to fully and properly fill the form with authorized signatures by the person sponsoring the visa applicant then notarized by the accredited authorities in Portugal is mandatory.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:

General The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is
                                                                                        not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels
                                                                                        one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the
                                                                                        picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning
                                                                                            will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                                    to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with. Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

Please note the authentication of signature can be done at the Portugal Embassy in Abu Dhabi or by authorise authorities only.

## Photo Specifications
Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes. Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
- Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note: If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time

                                                                        days between the date of application and date of travel. Applications will not be accepted if this requirement is not adhered to.
In addition to the above processing time please refer to Article 22:
Prior consultation of central authorities of other Member States
- A Member State may require the central authorities of other Member States to consult its central authorities during the examination of applications lodged by nationals of specific third countries or specific categories of such nationals. Such consultation shall not apply to applications for airport transit visas.
- The central authorities consulted shall reply definitively within seven calendar days after being consulted. The absence of a reply within this deadline shall mean that they have no grounds for objecting to the issuing of the visa.
- Member States shall notify the Commission of the introduction or withdrawal of the requirement of prior consultation before it becomes applicable. This information shall also be given within local Schengen cooperation in the jurisdiction concerned.
- The Commission shall inform Member States of such notifications.
- From the date of the replacement of the Schengen Consultation Network, as referred to in Article 46 of the VIS Regulation, prior consultation shall be carried out in accordance with Article 16(2) of that Regulation.
Click here for the list of Countries that article 22 apply to.

- Tourism (#)
- Business visit (#)
- Medical Treatment (#)
- Training Purpose (#)
- Airport Transit (#)
Please note:
For all the applicants holding a newly renewed UAE residence visa without the sticker on the passport, it is mandatory to provide a copy of the online document that certifies your UAE residence at the time of application and a copy of your Emirates ID.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission.

visa_types.name: National Visa
visa_types.overview:
Visas for visits exceeding three months, whether for study, training, working or residence, shall be national visas issued by Portuguese Embassy in accordance with its national legislation. These
                                                            types of visas are only valid for the national territory of the concerned country.
Portugal Embassy in UAE will require at least 90 days to process the Visa application after submission at VFS or after Embassy interview (if Embassy interview is required). However, this deadline
                                                            may be extended up to 60 days and even, exceptionally, 90 days, if a more detailed examination of your application and/or additional documents are required.

Please note:
For all the applicants holding a newly renewed UAE residence visa without the sticker on the passport, it is mandatory to provide a copy of the online document that certifies your UAE residence at the time of application and a copy of your Emirates ID.
